package: Goal 2 finite causal-patch / horizon-code toy model result index
scope: Results through cosmology Phase 31, with Goal 1 balanced-bridge theorem as seed

status_legend.exact_theorem: A theorem-level claim checked by symbolic/template reasoning and exact finite-prefix verification where applicable.
status_legend.exact_exhaustive_bounded_search: All objects in a stated finite bounded family are enumerated and verified exactly.
status_legend.exact_finite_certificate: A concrete finite construction or finite prefix is verified exactly.
status_legend.exploratory_frontier_evidence: Bounded frontier/search evidence that is exact for searched records but not exhaustive for a broad mathematical family.

definitions.definition: An n-qubit stabilizer subspace represented by Pauli generators, with exact diagnostics for logical basis, distance, entropy, region algebra, and erasure correctability.
Two stabilizer codes compared on entropy-visible and reconstruction-visible diagnostics.
A finite list of named physical-qubit regions attached to a code or code pair.
A named patch interpreted as an observer causal patch, usually observer_p or observer_q.
The named overlap region that must equal observer_p intersect observer_q under strict causal-patch gates.
The collection of bridge-pair qubits added by the balanced-bridge construction.
The causal union observer_p union observer_q.
Named patch entropies plus overlap, MI, CMI, and tripartite information data for patch pairs.
The tuple (logical_dim, center_dim, commutant_dim, reconstructs_all) for a region algebra.
Named erasure scenarios recording correctability for each code.
Whether the survivor complement reconstructs all logicals after an erasure.
Matching entropy/overlap data, horizon overlap, shared horizon algebra, observer reconstruction difference, matching erasure profile, and erasure algebra difference.
A certified transition graph or edge-filtered subgraph supporting exact rational channel rules.

claims.title: Balanced-bridge CSS separation family
claims.claim: For all bridge counts m, A_m and B_m have n=6+2m, k=1, d=2, matching labeled one- and two-qubit entropy diagnostics, no single-qubit non-central logical reconstruction, and distinct reconstruction/algebra profiles.
claims.scope: All m under the balanced-bridge generator rule.
claims.evidence_type: symbolic CSS support-mask proof plus exact prefix certificates

claims.title: Static balanced-bridge causal-patch atlas
claims.claim: Named patch entropy, overlap, MI, CMI, and I3 data match, and shared-horizon algebra matches, while observer-patch reconstructability differs.
claims.scope: Checked balanced-bridge prefixes for the source-aware atlas.

claims.title: Deterministic bridge growth increment laws
claims.claim: Bridge growth preserves causal-patch separation and has exact increment laws for observer entropy, observer-pair MI, private CMI, tripartite information, and witness algebra signatures.
claims.evidence_type: exact finite bridge-growth and erasure-channel certificate

claims.title: Source-aware covers recover lifted bridge slices
claims.claim: Generic bounded covers find the seed but not lifted bridge slices, while source-aware bridge atlases recover lifted sources under the same strict hit criteria.
claims.evidence_type: exact bounded search plus replayable frontier-cache records

claims.title: Distance-repaired non-CSS atlas recovery
claims.claim: Distance repair, low-order entropy matching, and strict causal-patch erasure semantics are separable; atlas-aware covers recover strict hits for repaired graph/CWS-like examples.
claims.scope: Phase 11/12 repaired graph/CWS-like sources and 525 atlas-aware repaired covers.
claims.evidence_type: exact rank-kernel diagnostics and bounded repaired-cover search

claims.title: Repaired cover dynamics and role-semantics comparison
claims.claim: A fixed repaired non-CSS code pair admits a cover flow from a near-miss to a strict atlas hit; role labels alone do not determine horizon semantics.
claims.scope: Phase 13 two-slice flow and Phase 14 bounded cover-transition graph.
claims.evidence_type: exact finite cover-flow and rank-kernel erasure certificate

claims.title: Mixed transition graph and exact rational channels
claims.claim: The mixed inner/outer graph has stable shared-horizon correctability and survivor fixed point while entropy breaks, operator collapse, and algebra monotonicity failures occur under certified transitions.
claims.scope: 49-node, 84-edge mixed transition graph and exact rational channels.
claims.evidence_type: exact finite transition graph and rational Markov/channel certificates

claims.title: Bounded exact channel-rule search and robust synthesis
claims.claim: Stationary bucket weights, absorbing classes, target signs, and Pareto frontiers depend on exact channel rules and substrates; robust synthesis gives audited no-go and yes-go target signs within a 27-rule language.
claims.scope: 27 positive symmetric edge-bonus rules, 7 descent rules, and four Phase 26 substrates.
claims.evidence_type: exact exhaustive bounded enumeration with rational certificates and audited feature predicates

claims.title: Substrate and cover co-design pressure tests
claims.claim: Some robust no-go signs are substrate- or cover-family artifacts, while others persist under stricter gates.
claims.scope: Phase 29 eight substrate families and Phase 30 selected strict/near-miss covers.
claims.evidence_type: exact bounded co-design enumeration with strict-vs-near-miss separation

claims.title: Strict repaired-cover exhaustive audit
claims.claim: Among 175 repaired private-full/shared-inner covers there are 66 raw hits, 8 strict hits, and 58 erasure-gate rejections. Entropy_break - full_semantics stays negative for all strict hits, while operator-related sign flips occur exactly when observer_p_private_blocks is nonempty.
claims.scope: All 175 Phase 12 private-full/shared-inner repaired covers and all eight strict hits.
claims.evidence_type: exact exhaustive bounded cover scan and exact robust-channel audit

claims.title: Extended source frontier evidence
claims.claim: CSS, graph/CWS-like, and shallow-encoder source records provide additional exact bounded evidence for entropy/reconstruction separation searches, but do not exhaust broad code families.
claims.evidence_type: bounded exact frontier records and cache replay, not global exhaustive proof

limitations: Bounded cover grammars and transition-rule families.
Role labels are not physical geometry by themselves.
The de Sitter analogy is diagnostic rather than literal.
Entropy matching is limited to specified patch or low-order diagnostics unless a certificate states otherwise.
next_research_directions: Move to tensor-network or holographic-code extensions.
Translate the package into a short paper-style note.
